subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus, reinforced by precise terminology and consistent signposting.
The research question is clearly stated, appropriately narrow, and maintained throughout the essay, guiding selection and critique of sources.
weaknesses: The methodology lacks a transparent search strategy (databases, keywords, inclusion/exclusion flowchart) limiting reproducibility.
Operational definitions for key variables are asserted briefly rather than fully justified in context, reducing methodological transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While two strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the top band descriptors, the research methodology strand falls into the mid-band (3-4) due to missing search strategy details. This mix justifies a score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates solid understanding of psychological concepts (e.g., recall bias, longitudinal design) and applies most sources appropriately.
Uses subject-specific terminology (e.g., logistic regression, construct validity, BDI) accurately in context.
weaknesses: Depth occasionally lapses when discussing neurological development and depressive symptomatology, preventing consistently sophisticated treatment.
Minor imprecisions in terminology (e.g., calling an observational study an âexperimentâ) and sporadic typographical errors reduce polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands sit squarely in the 3-4 band: good knowledge and mostly accurate terminology but lacking the sustained sophistication and precision required for the top band, warranting 4 out of 6.

strengths: Research draws on a broad set of peer-reviewed studies that are consistently relevant to the research question, demonstrating discernment in source selection.
Analysis identifies key biases (recall, sampling) and compares effect sizes, showing clear evaluation of strengths and limitations.
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative synthesis (e.g., meta-analytic metrics) and deeper theoretical integration across studies.
Discussion of contradictory evidence and weighting of confounders remains brief, limiting the depth of critical evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With research quality in the 7-9 band but analysis and evaluation in the 4-6 band, the overall performance aligns with the mid-band for Critical Thinking. A score of 6 out of 12 reflects solid but not fully comprehensive critical engagement.

strengths: Fully meets Extended Essay structural conventions: clear title page, abstracted word count, detailed table of contents, and logically numbered sections.
Layout considerations (APA references, bold headings) are correctly applied, facilitating navigation.
weaknesses: Minor formatting lapses (escaped backslashes in DOIs, occasional missing page numbers) prevent flawless presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required structural elements are present (top-band), but small formatting errors keep it from perfection. This justifies a score of 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Psychological terminologyâe.g., ârandom sampling,â âlogistic regression,â âconstruct validity,â âself-report bias,â and diagnostic tools such as BDI and CDIâis used mostly accurately and in context.  Minor lapses (occasional imprecise phrasing such as referring to âexperimentâ where the cited design is correlational) and sporadic typographical errors lower the overall polish, situating performance solidly in the 3-4 band rather than exhibiting the unwavering precision expected at 5-6.

Formatting is largely consistent: APA-style references, bolded section headings, and page indicators in the table of contents.  Minor issues persist (escaped backslashes in DOIs, occasional LaTeX artefacts, and page numbers not visible on every excerpt page).  These small lapses mean layout is correct overall but not flawless, warranting a low-top-band score.

Across the body of the essay the student demonstrates solid understanding of psychological concepts such as recall bias, longitudinal versus cross-sectional design, and socioeconomic confounding.  The chosen studies are generally appropriate, correctly summarized, and their findings linked to the argument.  Depth occasionally slips (e.g., brief treatment of neurological development or depressive symptomatology), preventing the consistently sophisticated treatment needed for the highest band.

The student outlines basic inclusion/exclusion criteria (studies that isolate IPV and control for confounders vs. those that do not), presents eight peer-reviewed sources, and explains the logic for designating four as âsupportâ and four as âcounter.â  However, there is no explicit search strategy, database list, or justification of how the final sample of papers was located from the wider literature, and operational definitions for key variables are sometimes briefly asserted rather than fully justified.  Overall the methodology is mostly complete but lacks the detail and transparent selection evidence required for the top band.

The student frames the social phenomenon and its significance with up-to-date prevalence statistics and a concise operational definition of intimate partner violence.  The research focus is reiterated throughout the introduction and linked to every subsequent section through clear headings and purposeful signposting.  Terminology is used accurately and the scope (children who witness IPV but are not direct victims) is unambiguous, giving the reader an immediate and accurate understanding of the essayâs purpose.

The essay draws on eight peer-reviewed empirical studies that are, for the most part, directly relevant to the research question.  Four âsupportâ studies offer strong methodological designs; four weaker studies are purposefully included to illustrate limitations, demonstrating discernment in source relevance.  The coverage is broad (different countries, sample sizes, and designs), and the student justifies relevance throughout, evidencing âgoodââthough not flawlessly comprehensiveâresearch quality.

The research question â âTo what extent does intimate partner violence affect the probability of a child to suffer from depression at some point in their life?â â is clearly stated, appropriately narrow, and consistently referenced in the analytic and discussion sections.  All study selection, critique, and synthesis are explicitly tied back to this question, demonstrating a sustained and effective focus across the essay.

The essay follows Extended Essay conventions with a title page, abstracted word count, detailed table of contents, logically numbered sections, and clear separation of introduction, analysis, discussion and conclusion.  All required structural elements are present and facilitate navigation, fully satisfying descriptors in the highest presentation band.

The discussion section weaves findings into a coherent argument that witnessing IPV elevates risk for depression, and evaluates methodological strengths and weaknesses (e.g., reliance on self-report, cultural bias, ethical constraints).  However, evaluation of contradictory evidence is brief, weighting of confounders is only partially developed, and recommendations for future research are stated in general terms.  The argument is reasoned and partially critical but not incisively analytical, meriting a mid-band score.

Analysis moves beyond description by identifying sampling bias, recall bias, and issues of construct validity for each study, and by comparing effect sizes where available.  Nevertheless, theoretical integration remains limited and quantitative synthesis is absent, leaving some conclusions only moderately supported.  The work therefore fits comfortably in the 4-6 band but lacks the consistently well-supported, multi-layered analysis characteristic of the 7-9 or 10-12 ranges.

annotations.comment: Report statistical metrics (e.g., p-values, confidence intervals, effect sizes) when stating significance to support your analytical assertions with quantitative rigor.
Cultural bias is acknowledged but unspecified; outline targeted sampling strategies or collaboration with diverse cultural groups to enhance generalizability.
The title is clear and focused, accurately communicating the investigationâs purpose and scope, providing the reader with an immediate understanding of the essayâs topic and direction.
The research question is stated concisely and precisely, establishing a clear and consistent focus that guides the entire discussion and analysis.
The word count is provided at the start of the document, satisfying the formatting requirement and demonstrating adherence to IB guidelines.
The Table of Contents is presented clearly with bold headings and page numbers, facilitating navigation and meeting structural conventions.
All required sections are listed with accurate pagination, demonstrating a well-organized structure that aligns with Extended Essay formatting standards.

This opening paragraph covers context, definition, and research aim in a single block; consider separating background, gap identification, and research purpose into distinct paragraphs for enhanced clarity.
While the operational definition of IPV is cited, expanding on theoretical underpinnings or conceptual frameworks would deepen the readerâs understanding of its applicability.
Using U.S.-only prevalence statistics limits generalizability; incorporating global or cross-cultural data would strengthen the contextual foundation of your investigation.

The criteria for selecting support and counter studies are outlined but the search strategy (databases, keywords, inclusion/exclusion flowchart) is omitted; adding this would enhance methodological transparency.
Declaring no counter-argument studies and then including weaker research appears ad hoc; provide a more rigorous justification for inclusion criteria to avoid selection bias.
The Asfour et al. study is described as an âexperiment,â but its design is observational case-control; clarify terminology and ensure accurate categorization of research designs.
The identification of recall bias alongside random sampling as a strength demonstrates effective critical evaluation of methodological strengths and limitations.
The survey methodology is well described, but omits response rate and discussion of potential non-response bias; include these details to strengthen validity.
The recruitment via social workers likely introduces selection bias; discuss how this may impact generalizability and how it could have been mitigated.
The Childhood Depression Inventory is cited but its reliability and validity are not discussed; include psychometric properties to reinforce methodological credibility.
As a cross-sectional survey, the Nguyen et al. design cannot support causal claims; revise language to reflect correlation rather than causation.
Having parents read questionnaires to young children introduces potential interviewer and social desirability biases; describe any controls or alternative measures to address this.
The discussion of memory recall bias in child reporting shows strong critical thinking and an awareness of methodological limitations.
Attrition in the cohort study is noted, but there is no comparison of baseline characteristics between retained and dropped participants; include this to assess attrition bias.
The cutoff appears as HTML <math> rather than LaTeX; replace with $$\ge8$$ to ensure correct rendering and consistency.
Chan et al.âs path analysis is mentioned, but mediator variables, estimation techniques, and model fit indices are not described; include these for analytical transparency.
The BDI-II cutoff of 14 lacks citation; reference a validation study or the scaleâs manual to justify this threshold.
Including socio-demographic predictors such as maternal smoking and family structure demonstrates nuanced understanding of potential confounders.
The sample selection bias discussion is generic; quantify the dropout rate and analyze how excluded participants differ from the retained sample.
The discussion remains largely descriptive; integrating a theoretical framework (for example, ecological systems theory) would deepen analytical insight.
The claim of economic privilege is unsupported by descriptive statistics; include a demographic table to substantiate sample characteristics.
Modifying the CES-D timeframe alters its psychometric properties; cite validation research to support this adaptationâs reliability and validity.
Noting low variance explained is insightful; propose exploring additional predictors (e.g., resilience factors or family support) to improve model fit in future research.
Title italicization and authorâampersand spacing vary across citations; standardize these elements to comply with APA formatting requirements.
Future research recommendations are general; suggest a specific prospective longitudinal design with standardized measures to reduce recall bias and enhance rigor.
Ethical considerations are discussed without reference to formal guidelines; cite relevant APA or WHO ethics frameworks to strengthen evaluative depth.
Several DOI entries contain escaped characters or inconsistent hyperlink formatting; ensure proper APA-style DOI presentation throughout the references.
Ensure consistent spacing around initials and ampersands in author lists to maintain uniformity and adhere strictly to APA style.
